acceptance: 1. cli/internal/triage package exists with TriagePolicy, FailureMode, Action, and Decide(). 2. Default policy implements BLOCK ladder: re_attempt → escalate_tier → needs_human. 3. .ddx/config.yaml triage: block accepted by schema and unmarshals into ResolvedConfig. 4. Unit tests cover ladder progression, default for non-BLOCK modes, config override of default policy.
description:
Foundation for the auto-triage feature (parent: ddx-3c154349). Build the standalone triage decision module that the drain loop will call after each terminal event, plus its config schema. No loop integration in this bead — that lands in a follow-up child once this module is in place.

Scope:
- New package cli/internal/triage/ with TriagePolicy struct, FailureMode enum (review_block, lock_contention, execution_failed, no_changes), Action enum (re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, retry_with_backoff, file_followup, needs_human), and Decide(beadID, mode, history) Action method.
- Default policy mirrors AC#3 of parent: BLOCK→re_attempt_with_context (1st), escalate_tier (2nd), needs_human (3rd). Other modes get sensible defaults.
- Config block 'triage:' added to .ddx/config.yaml schema and ResolvedConfig (cli/internal/config/). Optional; defaults applied when absent.
- Unit tests for the policy engine: ladder progression for BLOCK, default for other modes, config override path.

Out of scope:
- Wiring this into execute_bead_loop.go (next child).
- Lock contention retry mechanics (handled by sibling lock bead + next child).
- Reviewer-pairing-degraded surfacing (next child).
title: Triage policy framework: config schema + decision engine

acceptance: 1. Lock contention errors classified and routed to retry_with_backoff with at least 3 backoff steps before escalation. 2. execution_failed enters triage and produces escalate_tier or needs_human depending on attempt count. 3. no_changes with a rationale that does not cite AC items results in a clarification follow-up bead being filed (and the original closed). 4. Tests cover lock-error retry success, lock-error backoff exhaustion, execution_failed escalation, no_changes follow-up.
description:
Third child of ddx-3c154349. Depends on the triage framework child. Covers the remaining failure modes.

Scope:
- Lock contention: detect 'Unable to create .git/index.lock' / staging-tracker lock errors in attempt outcomes. Triage action: retry_with_backoff (e.g. 30s, 90s, 300s) within the same drain pass before unclaiming. Sibling bead handles the underlying lock fix; this bead wires the retry decision.
- execution_failed (substantive, not infrastructure): triage maps to escalate_tier per the existing S10 ladder; on exhaustion → needs_human. Distinguish from already-handled escalatable statuses by treating execution_failed as an explicit triage entry-point.
- no_changes (NoChangesRationale present): triage decides whether the bead AC is genuinely satisfied (close already_satisfied — current behavior) or whether to file a clarification follow-up bead (when rationale doesn't quote AC items).
- Tests for: lock-error → retry succeeds on second attempt; lock-error → escalate after backoff exhaustion; execution_failed → escalate_tier; no_changes with weak rationale → file follow-up.

Out of scope:
- The actual git-lock infrastructure fix (sibling bead).
- Review BLOCK/REQUEST_CHANGES (sibling child).

acceptance: 1. Loop calls triage.Decide() after VerdictBlock and VerdictRequestChanges branches in execute_bead_loop.go. 2. BLOCK ladder progression works: 1st BLOCK → re-attempt; 2nd → tier escalation hint applied; 3rd → needs_human label + triage-decision event. 3. review-pairing-degraded events found in bead history are passed into the decision context. 4. Tests for BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate at 2nd attempt, BLOCK→needs_human at 3rd, and review-pairing-degraded biases toward re-attempt.
description:
Second of three children of ddx-3c154349. Depends on the triage policy framework child landing first.

Scope:
- In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, after the VerdictBlock and VerdictRequestChanges branches that already record events and reopen, call into triage.Decide(beadID, mode=review_block, history) to choose the next action.
- Track the per-bead BLOCK count by reading prior 'review' events with Summary='BLOCK' from the bead store. No new persistent state needed.
- Implement the three actions for review_block:
  - re_attempt_with_context: leave bead reopened (current behavior) so next drain pass picks it up; ensure the prompt assembler picks up the BLOCK rationale (already in bead notes via Reopen()).
  - escalate_tier: set a metadata hint or tier-pin so the next attempt runs at a higher tier (use existing escalation.ModelTier mechanism).
  - needs_human: add 'needs_human' label to the bead and emit a triage-decision event.
- Surface review-pairing-degraded events in the triage decision (AC#6 of parent): when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer, prefer re_attempt_with_context with a fresh reviewer pair rather than escalating.

Out of scope:
- Lock-contention retry path (next child).
- no_changes / execution_failed triage paths (this child is review-only).

acceptance:
1. GraphQL resolver in cli/internal/server/graphql/ no longer takes WorkingDir as a struct field. WorkingDir comes from request context via a context key (e.g., ctxutil.WorkingDir(ctx)).
2. All resolver methods (DocumentByPath, Beads, ExecRuns, etc.) read WorkingDir from context instead of r.WorkingDir.
3. The scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route injects the project's WorkingDir into context before invoking the resolver.
4. The trusted /graphql route injects s.WorkingDir into context (preserving backward compat).
5. cli/internal/server/server.go:4701 resolver construction stops setting WorkingDir.
6. Tests: existing TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_DocumentByPath_OnlyReturnsScopedProjectDocs still passes; existing TestGraphQL_LegacyRoute_StillWorks still passes.
7. New test: TestResolver_UsesContextWorkingDir — directly invokes resolver with context carrying a WorkingDir; asserts data scoped accordingly.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
description:
LAYER 2 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. LAYER 1 (ddx-4c51d33e) ships the scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route as the immediate security fix. LAYER 2 refactors the underlying resolver so the existing /graphql endpoint can also safely serve multiple projects.

PROBLEM
The GraphQL resolver is constructed once with WorkingDir as a struct field (cli/internal/server/server.go:4701 → cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_documents.go:26 etc.). The field is immutable per request. LAYER 1 works around this by reconstructing the resolver per scoped request, but the original /graphql route still has the structural issue.

LAYER 2 SCOPE
Refactor the GraphQL resolver to take WorkingDir from REQUEST CONTEXT, not a struct field. Thread project context through all resolvers via Go context.Context.

This makes both routes (/graphql and /api/projects/{project}/graphql) project-aware uniformly. The scoped middleware injects the WorkingDir; the unscoped trusted route falls back to s.WorkingDir.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new GraphQL queries (out of scope)
- Reshaping the schema
- LAYER 3 (Bead/Execution Node(id) resolution)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) — LAYER 1 establishes the scoped route mechanism; LAYER 2 generalizes the resolver.
title: graphql LAYER 2: refactor resolver to take WorkingDir from request context (replaces struct field)

2. ADR explicitly evaluates Proposals A, B, C (or articulates a fourth) and documents the chosen design with rationale.
3. ADR includes the worker-server API contract: full request/response schemas for register, heartbeat, next-bead, event, result, disconnect.
4. ADR includes sequence diagrams (text/mermaid) for: worker registration, bead claim, execute+report, server-restart recovery, graceful shutdown.
5. ADR includes a compatibility analysis: what existing tests break, what behaviors migrate, what's a breaking change for operators.
6. ADR specifies how --local mode collapses into the same code path (in-process API impl).
7. ADR specifies sequencing relative to: ddx-29058e2a (spec unification), ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor children, ddx-dc157075 (stay-alive — likely subsumed).
8. ADR includes implementation roadmap as an ordered list (with proposed bead titles, NOT actual filed beads — those come after ADR review).
9. FEAT-006 or new FEAT amended to describe the worker contract (whichever is more appropriate per existing HELIX layout).
10. Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.
description:
Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout
title: TD: client-server worker architecture — workers as long-lived API clients; server restart preserves in-flight work

acceptance: 1. rm -rf .agents/skills/cli/ .claude/skills/cli/ (or whatever specific subpaths the sub-agent identified; verify with git status that they're untracked before deleting). 2. routinglint analyzer confirmed wired into 'make lint' (and lefthook pre-commit if applicable); CI fails on AdaptiveMinTier reintroduction in any cli/ source file. Test: temporarily reintroduce 'AdaptiveMinTier' as an identifier in a throwaway commit, run 'make lint', confirm failure with clear message; revert. 3. Manual verification: 'ddx work --harness claude --once' spawns a worker; output does NOT contain 'adaptive min-tier' string. Save the output to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/verification.txt. 4. grep -rn 'adaptive.min.tier\|AdaptiveMinTier\|--no-adaptive\|--adaptive-min-tier-window' cli/ (excluding .agents/.claude paths) returns 0 hits beyond the lint-guard reference itself. 5. ddx doc audit clean.
description:
Sub-agent investigation confirms adaptive-min-tier was already removed in commit 9d988853 (2026-04-29, bead ddx-3bd7396a). Function, both flags (--no-adaptive-min-tier, --adaptive-min-tier-window), printf, call site, config schema fields, tests — all gone. routinglint analyzer at cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go:77 forbids reintroduction of the AdaptiveMinTier identifier.

User was seeing the printf message because they were running a stale binary (alpha6 or earlier predating the removal). After alpha7 install (this session), the message no longer prints.

This bead is residual hygiene:

1. Delete the stale .agents/skills/cli/ and .claude/skills/cli/ mirror trees that still contain the old adaptive-min-tier code + printf. Per locked decision: they appear in 'git status' as untracked (??), they're local scratch from prior dev work, NOT shippable. Delete them. routinglint analyzer prevents reintroduction.

2. Verify the routinglint analyzer is wired into the default 'make lint' / pre-commit path so any future reintroduction is caught at PR time, not after-the-fact at runtime.

3. Capture before/after evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ showing the message no longer prints from a fresh worker spawn.
title: verify adaptive-min-tier removal stuck + delete stale .agents/.claude mirror trees

acceptance: 1. For each of the 4 functions: trace the bead that introduced it; verify the bead's AC still describes desired behavior (or document obsolescence). 2. For each: either wire into execute_bead_loop.go (if still relevant) OR delete the function + its tests + reopen the originating bead with the obsolescence rationale. 3. After wiring: spawn a worker, drain a few beads, verify execution evidence shows the new code paths firing. 4. Save the audit findings + decisions per function to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/wire-decisions.md. 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. The 5 originating beads (ddx-da11a34a, ddx-aede917d, ddx-cfedee8e, etc.) verified via test execution showing their behavior is now active in production.
description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: Superseded by ddx-83440482 (REACH-BACKFILL): the 4 unwired functions become a subset of the systematic production-reachability sweep once REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) + REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8) land. Resolving them as part of the sweep ensures the same gate prevents recurrence.
title: audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads

acceptance: 1. All child beads closed. 2. .ddx/beads.jsonl can be kept under a configurable size threshold via active+archive split. 3. Existing tooling (ddx bead list/show/ready/blocked, ddx work) continues to work transparently across active and archived beads. 4. bd/br interchange tests still green. 5. Migration tool exists and runs cleanly on the current 5.4MB beads.jsonl.
description:
ADR-004 (Accepted) decided to generalize .ddx/beads.jsonl into a named-collection abstraction with separate archive and attachment storage, but the implementation hasn't shipped. Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

References: ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004, recent observation that beads.jsonl crossed the 5MB lefthook threshold during the 2026-05 redesign drain.

What is done:
- cli/internal/federation/ package exists with state.go, persistence.go, state_test.go
  (introduced in commit 0dd3cead "feat(federation): add internal/federation state
  types + persistence [ddx-1bbd1096]", which is an ancestor of base-rev 3f97b3f6).
- state.go defines:
    * FederationRegistry { SchemaVersion, Spokes }
    * SpokeRecord { NodeID, Name, URL, DDxVersion, SchemaVersion, Capabilities,
      RegisteredAt, LastHeartbeat, Status }
    * SpokeStatus enum covering registered/active/stale/offline/degraded with
      Valid() guard.
    * NewRegistry, FindSpoke, UpsertSpoke, RemoveSpoke, SetStatus,
      ReconcileLiveness reconciliation helper.
- persistence.go implements:
    * DefaultStatePath honoring XDG_DATA_HOME, default ~/.local/share/ddx/federation-state.json.
    * LoadState/LoadStateFrom: missing-file → fresh registry; malformed JSON →
      quarantine to ".corrupt-<ts>" and return fresh registry; pre-versioned
      files default to CurrentSchemaVersion (migration stub).
    * SaveState/SaveStateTo: tmpfile + fsync + chmod 0600 + atomic rename, with
      a process-wide write mutex.
- state_test.go covers all AC tests and they pass:
    * TestRoundTrip — load/save round-trip + schema_version present in JSON +
      0600 file mode.
    * TestLoadMissingFileReturnsFreshRegistry — missing-file fresh-init.
    * TestLoadMalformedFileRecovers — malformed-file recovery + quarantine.
    * TestSchemaMigrationStub — schema migration stub (legacy → current).
    * TestStatusTransitions — registered/active/stale + offline/degraded
      stickiness + invalid-status rejection.
    * TestUpsertAndRemove — invariants on UpsertSpoke / RemoveSpoke.
    * TestConcurrentWriteSafety — 16 concurrent SaveStateTo, no leftover tmpfiles.
    * TestDefaultStatePathHonorsXDG — XDG_DATA_HOME and ~/.local/share fallback.

acceptance:
1. Investigation phase. Read fizeau v0.10.3 source (cli/go.mod pin) and confirm whether explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower actually skips routing OR runs routing within the harness. Evidence committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md with file:line citations into fizeau's source. Outcome A: gap confirmed → continue; Outcome B: gap doesn't exist → close bead as already-resolved with the report as evidence.

2. (Outcome A only) Upstream fizeau change: file an issue/PR against github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau so that explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower triggers the routing engine within that harness's eligible models. For Class 2 harnesses (AutoRoutingEligible=false: gemini/opencode/pi), the engine returns an explicit "no auto-resolution available for harness=X" error, NOT silent empty Model.

3. (Outcome A only) Bump fizeau in cli/go.mod once the upstream change ships.

4. Integration test (in fizeau, or DDx-side stub if upstream test coverage is sufficient): TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness — assert ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"claude", Model:"", Profile:"code-economy"} → response RoutingActual.Model is non-empty AND is a member of the claude family per catalog.

5. Companion negative test: TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly — assert empty Model + empty Profile/MinPower returns a clear "under-specified routing" error instead of silently empty Model.

6. End-to-end verification (after cfedee8e/C9 also landed): ddx work --harness=claude on a small queue. Assert via:
   - .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json: Model field non-empty after first attempt
   - .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json: routing_decision evidence shows resolved model from the claude family
   - ddx server workers show <id>: Model field non-empty
   - NOT via process-list inspection

7. Use current catalog model names in tests/assertions: opus-4.7, sonnet-4.6, claude-haiku-5.5 (NOT stale Haiku/Sonnet/Opus literals). Fizeau may normalize to family aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) for the actual --model arg via service_models.go:164-185.

8. NO local-config "fallback when fizeau unavailable" path. Empty fizeau response = fail the attempt; DDx retry policy escalates next attempt.

9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

10. Documentation: code comment on fizeau Execute call site (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go) noting that empty Model + Profile/MinPower is the well-formed request shape and what fizeau is expected to return.
description:
Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == ""). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model="", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.

OBSERVED
- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg
- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude
- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model="" (operator sees "claude" but not which Claude model)

SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY

This bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.

INVESTIGATION FIRST
Confirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == "". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.

UPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)
Modify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the "Harness != '' means skip routing" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.

For Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear "no auto-resolution available" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.

DDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)
Once the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.

NOT IN SCOPE
- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)
- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)
- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)
- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)

WHY P1 (was P0)
Demoted because:
- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue
- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land
- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change
- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job

DEPS
- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on
title: fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)

acceptance: --hub= flag registers on startup. Re-start with same node_id is idempotent (replaces, not duplicates). Heartbeat interval 30s ±jitter. URL change between restarts triggers re-registration with new URL. Graceful shutdown sends deregister (best-effort, no error if hub down). hub_spoke role works (both flags). /api/node exposes federation_role. Integration tests cover lifecycle: register → heartbeat → URL-change → deregister.
description: Implement spoke-side federation lifecycle on ddx-server: --hub=<host> flag triggers registration on startup. Idempotent register-on-start using stable node_id. Heartbeat every 30s with jitter (avoid synchronized beats); spoke marked stale at 2m of missed beats. Detect own URL change between starts and re-register with updated URL. Deregister on graceful shutdown (best-effort). Both --hub-mode and --hub= can be set on the same server (hub_spoke role). Expose node.federation_role in /api/node response: standalone | hub | spoke | hub_spoke. Integration tests with in-process hub. See /tmp/story-14-final.md 'Authority' and 'Discovery' sections.

description:
Today reviewer defaults to implementer harness (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:521) — directly violating R4 (different reviewer). Build the reviewer ExecuteRequest with: Role='reviewer'; SAME CorrelationID as the implementer call (joins them in events / session log / aggregations); MinPower = implementer.RoutingActual.Power + 1.

The 4 ACs span federation infrastructure that does not yet exist (mutation routing — current federation in cli/internal/federation/fanout.go supports only query fan-out, not coordinator→client mutation routing), identity propagation/dual authorization, a CI lint tool, and an E2E integration test. Each piece is non-trivial on its own (~200-400 LOC, distinct subsystems).

Decomposition:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers (requireTrusted + CSRF/Origin). Independently shippable today; no dependency on routing work.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation). Adds RouteMutationToProjectOwner + ForwardMutation + hub forwarding handler.
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization (origin + coordinator). Depends on S15-7b.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test — coordinator→client operatorPromptSubmit with identity attestation. Depends on S15-7b and S15-7c.

Dependency edges added via `ddx bead dep add`. Parent bead ddx-256af8b5 annotated with --notes pointer to children. No code changes were made in this attempt.

acceptance:
1. Single struct ExecuteLoopSpec in cli/internal/agent/ (or sub-package) with JSON tags. Includes ALL fields currently distributed across cobra extracts, server request struct, ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, and runWorker consumption: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, plus SpecVersion int. Audit current code: any field in cobra/server/spec/runWorker NOT in this list = bug, must add.

2. SEPARATE DispatchOptions struct holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) that must NOT appear on ExecuteLoopSpec or be persisted to spec.json. parseExecuteLoopSpec returns (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — single cobra→spec conversion; called only from cobra RunE; never re-implemented.

3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() exist as methods. Lifecycle:
   (a) parseExecuteLoopSpec → cobra defaults applied via cobra binding
   (b) client-side: Validate() before HTTP submit
   (c) server-side: decode → ApplyDefaults() → Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop
   (d) GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through the SAME StartExecuteLoop (no parallel construction)
   (e) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran)

4. JSON wire format for time.Duration uses string representation (e.g. "30s", "5m"). UnmarshalJSON tolerates both string and numeric (nanoseconds) for backward-compat. Custom Duration type or json.RawMessage→time.ParseDuration acceptable.

5. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go execute-loop / work RunE: constructs (spec, dispatch) via parseExecuteLoopSpec; dispatches: dispatch.Local → runExecuteLoopInline(spec); else httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec). NO positional-param flag passing remains.

6. httpSubmitExecuteLoop marshals the spec directly as the HTTP request body (json.Marshal(spec)). No intermediate map[string]interface{}.

7. Server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes directly INTO ExecuteLoopSpec (no parallel request struct). The previously hand-maintained struct in server.go:2246-2260 is DELETED. ApplyDefaults + Validate run before StartExecuteLoop.

8. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) DELETES its parallel request struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal. Goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop path as the REST handler.

9. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in workers.go is REPLACED by (or aliased to) ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json on disk uses the unified struct.

10. runWorker(spec) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD (with default fallback when zero), spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, spec.FromRev — no hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD anywhere except the fallback case.

11. Reflection-backed round-trip test (TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection): uses reflect over ExecuteLoopSpec's exported fields; for each, sets a non-zero/non-default value, marshals, unmarshals on the server side, asserts the value survives into spec.json AND into the runWorker-visible spec. Adding a future field = automatic test coverage (no row-by-row maintenance). Test FAILS if any exported field is missing source/sink coverage.

12. Specific OpaquePassthrough assertion: server-submitted ddx work --harness claude persists opaque_passthrough:true and skips BOTH validation gates (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773). Test names both line ranges.

13. Backward compatibility: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ continue to load. Specifically: legacy fields min_tier, max_tier, and any other historical fields are tolerated and ignored (json.Decoder default behavior; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads). Spot-check: load .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json without error.

14. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field added. Server rejects with 400 + capability list when client SpecVersion > server-supported. Same client/server version always works. Document the stance in code comment.

15. Regression test: --local path is unchanged in behavior. Existing local-path tests pass.

16. Manual verification: `ddx work --harness claude` (no --local) on a small queue completes at least one bead end-to-end without harness-rejection error.

17. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

DEPENDENCIES
- Recommend landing AFTER ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive). If landing in parallel, coordinate execute_bead_loop.go merges.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker) is independent — LabelFilter stays string.
- REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744): new package added as entry-root.
description:
The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

WIRED-IN: integration test (TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt) issues cancel against a worker mid-flight on a real (or scripted-claude-stub-with-realistic-timing) attempt; asserts the actual subprocess receives the cancel signal and aborts at next safe point. Not just a stub-attempt cancel.
description: Server endpoint /api/beads/<id>/cancel writes extra.cancel-requested:true. Worker mid-attempt poll (10s default) re-reads bead extra; on cancel-requested:true, abort at next safe point (between LLM turns / git ops) with preserved_for_review reason 'operator_cancel'. Idempotent: second cancel writes cancel-honored:true silently. ~150 LOC.

description:
Refactor cli/internal/bead/store.go (1687 lines, currently hard-codes .ddx/beads.jsonl) to accept a logical collection name. Introduce a CollectionRef abstraction so the same Store type can back 'beads' (active queue) and future collections like 'beads-archive', 'exec-runs', 'agent-sessions'. JSONL backend resolves a collection to a file under .ddx/<name>.jsonl. External (bd/br) backend keeps current behavior for the default 'beads' collection; non-default collections may stub out for this slice. No user-facing behavior change yet — default callers (ddx bead list/show/etc.) still operate on 'beads'.

References: ADR-004 §Decision items 1-3, store.go, backend.go, backend_external.go.
title: Generalize bead store to named-collection abstraction (ADR-004 step 1)

acceptance: 1. Loop has triage step after each attempt's terminal event. 2. Per-failure-mode triage policy in .ddx/config.yaml. 3. Triage outcomes (re-attempt-with-context, escalate-tier, needs_human) align exactly with TD-NNN TriageContract (per ddx-673833f4). **No new persisted statuses introduced — all triage state lives in labels (e.g., triage:needs-human) and events per bd/br compatibility (ADR-004).** 4. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier, then add 'triage:needs-human' label. 5. Lock-error handling delegates to ddx-da11a34a's implementation; this bead does NOT duplicate retry/backoff logic. Triage policy entry for lock errors invokes the lock-bead's published API. 6. Tests: BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate, lock-error→delegates-to-lock-bead, no_changes→re-evaluate. 7. Out of scope: rate-limit handling, quota-pause handling, lock-retry implementation, status-enum modification. 8. needs_human stays a LABEL per current code (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:19-23) and TD's resolution. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Currently when a bead hits a post-merge review BLOCK, the drain loop emits the BLOCK event, leaves the bead open, and moves to the next ready bead. The BLOCK then sits in the queue waiting for a human. Same for many substantive execution_failed cases — no automated triage.

This bead handles BLOCK + substantive failures only. Two SEPARATE beads cover related-but-distinct concerns:
- ddx-aede917d: drain pause/resume on quota exhaustion (Fizeau-side state, DDx consumes typed error)
- ddx-c6e3db02: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)

Failure modes triaged HERE:
- post-merge review BLOCK → either (a) re-attempt-with-context (BLOCK reasons embedded in next prompt), (b) escalate to next tier in the S10_2 ladder, or (c) file follow-up bead capturing BLOCK reasons + mark original needs_human
- 'staging tracker: Unable to create .git/index.lock' (git contention — see  / ddx-da11a34a) → retry with backoff after lock clears
- substantive execution_failed (probe_ok+exec_fail; agent finished but produced no useful diff or wrong shape) → escalate per S10 ladder
- 'no_changes' (agent decided nothing to do) → re-evaluate against the bead AC; if still seems valid, file a clarification follow-up rather than mark already_satisfied

Triage policy is config-driven, per-failure-mode mapping in .ddx/config.yaml triage:. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier on second BLOCK, then needs_human on third.

NOT in scope: rate-limit handling (ddx-c6e3db02) and quota-pause handling (ddx-aede917d) — those are separate beads with their own infrastructure.

Children 2 and 3 depend on child 1.

description:
Slice 2 of parent ddx-a946c744. Wire the checks runner (built in child slice ddx-aee651ec) into the execute-bead loop as a pre_merge gate, add the checks_bypass bead annotation with event recording, and add e2e tests.

DEPENDS ON
- ddx-aee651ec (checks protocol package + ddx ac CLI)

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design. This slice integrates the protocol package into the merge flow and adds the bypass mechanism.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — call cli/internal/checks runner for all applicable checks BEFORE the merge-back step; on any status=block/error, abort merge, preserve worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions, write evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, record event
- cli/internal/bead schema — checks_bypass annotation: list of {name, reason, bead}; missing reason -> bypass rejected loudly; on bypass, named check is skipped and a bypass event is recorded with the reason
- E2E test 1: fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with script that always returns status=block; ddx try on a no-op bead aborts merge, preserves worktree, evidence file present, event recorded
- E2E test 2: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on bead -> merge proceeds, bypass event recorded
- Tests under cli/internal/agent/ and/or cli/cmd/ following existing execute_bead_*_test.go patterns

OUT OF SCOPE
- New checks for any specific language (separate REACH-* beads)
title: checks integration: pre_merge hook in execute-bead loop + checks_bypass annotation + e2e

acceptance:
1. New route POST /api/projects/{project}/graphql registered via scoped() middleware in cli/internal/server/server.go (parallel to existing /graphql trusted route).
2. The scoped route resolves the {project} path parameter via existing project context (workingDirForRequest or equivalent); rejects unregistered project with 404.
3. The route's GraphQL handler uses the request's project's WorkingDir (NOT s.WorkingDir) for all resolvers invoked through it. Implementation may either (a) reconstruct resolver per-request with the right WorkingDir, or (b) thread the WorkingDir through context — either is acceptable for LAYER 1.
4. Existing /graphql trusted route behavior unchanged (regression test).
5. Test: TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_DocumentByPath_OnlyReturnsScopedProjectDocs — register two projects A and B; query DocumentByPath via /api/projects/B/graphql; assert only B's documents are returned.
6. Test: TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_RejectsUnregisteredProject — query against /api/projects/unknown/graphql returns 404.
7. Test: TestGraphQL_LegacyRoute_StillWorks — assert /graphql still serves the startup project's data unchanged.
8. Manual verification: start server in project A, register project B, navigate to /nodes/local/projects/B in the frontend; verify documents/beads/executions shown via the scoped endpoint are B's, not A's. (May require a frontend one-line change to query the scoped endpoint when projectId is present; if so, include in this bead.)
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Followup beads filed for LAYER 2 and LAYER 3 (IDs noted in this bead's notes).
description:
LAYER 1 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. The full LAYER 1/2/3 plan was originally one bead; codex queue-hygiene review (2026-05-03) approved decomposition because LAYER 1 alone closes the HIGH-severity security gap and should ship without waiting for LAYERS 2-3.

ROOT CAUSE
The DDx server is single-project at initialization but multi-project-aware at runtime. HTTP REST endpoints properly enforce project boundaries via scoped()/legacy() middleware (cli/internal/server/server.go:712-719) and workingDirForRequest() (cli/internal/server/server.go:651-656). MCP tools resolve project correctly via mcpResolveWorkingDir() (cli/internal/server/server.go:590-612).

GraphQL bypasses ALL of this. The resolver is initialized once at cli/internal/server/server.go:4701 with WorkingDir: s.WorkingDir (server's startup project) and that field is immutable. The /graphql route is registered as `trusted("POST /graphql", ...)` not `scoped()`, so it cannot accept a {project} path parameter. The HIGH-severity exposure is at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_documents.go:26 — DocumentByPath always reads from r.WorkingDir.

LAYER 1 SCOPE
Add a parallel route /api/projects/{project}/graphql:
- Registered via scoped() middleware
- Resolves the {project} path param via existing project context infrastructure
- Reconstructs the GraphQL resolver with the request's project's WorkingDir per request (or threads it via context to the existing resolver)
- Existing /graphql endpoint behavior unchanged for backward compat

This is the immediate security fix. Frontend routes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/... can switch their GraphQL client to point at the scoped endpoint when projectId is present.

NOT IN LAYER 1 (deferred to LAYERS 2 + 3 — separate beads)
- LAYER 2: refactor resolver to take WorkingDir from request context across ALL resolvers (not just per-route reconstruction). Allows the existing /graphql endpoint to safely serve multiple projects.
- LAYER 3: implement Bead/Execution resolution in Node(id) Relay lookup with project-aware lookups (resolver_node.go:86-101 currently returns nil for bead IDs).

WHY DECOMPOSE
- LAYER 1 ships the security fix in one bead worth of work
- LAYER 2 is a larger refactor that doesn't need to block the security fix
- LAYER 3 is completeness; not security-critical
- All three could land independently if needed (LAYER 2/3 don't depend on each other)

3. Go testing helper testutil.NewFixtureRepo(t *testing.T, profile string) (projectPath string) wraps the script; auto-cleanup via t.Cleanup(); usable from any cli/internal/*/test file.
4. At least one existing integration test migrates to use the helper (e.g., one of the cross-project tests in graphql_scoped_route_test.go) — proves the API works in practice.
5. Documentation: short README at scripts/build-fixture-repo.md describing the profiles + usage; one paragraph in CLAUDE.md or testing.md pointing at the helper.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
7. Manual verification: bash scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-fixture-test --profile standard && cd /tmp/ddx-fixture-test && ddx bead ready # shows the seeded beads.
description:
Multiple test/demo workflows need a clean ddx-initialized git repo without polluting this main project. Today each test creates its own t.TempDir() fixture; integration tests do ad-hoc setup; acceptance demos have nowhere to go.

USE CASES (current + planned)
- ADR-022 acceptance demo (Stage 4 of design-phase plan): run 2 workers, restart server mid-execution, verify worker resilience without polluting the ddx repo
- Integration tests that need a real .ddx/ directory + bead store + workers/ + executions/
- bd/br interchange tests
- Cross-project leak tests (need >1 project — already partially used in ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1 tests)
- Manual operator walkthroughs / demos for new features
- "What does ddx look like in a fresh project?" docs

DESIGN OPTIONS

Option 1: A SCRIPT that builds a fixture repo on demand
- scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
- Creates a tmp git repo, runs ddx init, optionally adds N seed beads, optionally registers projects
- Tests call it via testing.T helper; demos call it from the operator's shell
- Cleanup is operator/test responsibility (rm -rf <dest>)

Option 2: A FIXTURE-REPO BINARY (ddx fixture create)
- ddx fixture create <dest> --profile standard
- Same outputs as Option 1 but discoverable via --help
- Could be a hidden subcommand under ddx (Hidden: true) since it's dev infrastructure
- Uniform with rest of CLI

Option 3: GO TESTING HELPER (testing-only, not exposed to operator)
- testutil.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile) → projectPath
- Auto-cleanup via t.Cleanup()
- Doesn't help acceptance demos but cleanest for unit/integration tests

RECOMMEND: Hybrid. Option 1 (script) for demo + manual use; Option 3 (Go helper) wraps the script for tests so cleanup is automatic. Option 2 deferred unless operators ask for it.

PROFILES TO SUPPORT (initial)
- minimal: empty .ddx/, no beads
- standard: .ddx/ + 5 mixed-priority sample beads + sample personas + sample skills
- multi-project: 2 registered projects, each with their own beads (for cross-project tests)
- federated: hub + spoke setup (for ADR-007 federation tests)

NOT IN SCOPE
- Mocking the agent service (separate concern; tests use stub fizeau already)
- Pre-populating with real LLM execution traces (deterministic test runs use fixtures, not live LLMs)
- Auto-cleanup beyond t.Cleanup() (operators run rm -rf)

DEPS
- Independent; can land anytime.
title: infra: reusable clean fixture repo for tests + acceptance demos

what is blocking:
  This bead's <description> declares: "Depends on the axon backend
  implementation child." The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was decomposed
  on 2026-05-02 23:10 into seven children. The migration tool sits
  downstream of:

    - ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration         (status: ?)
    - ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface (status: ?)
    - ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker     (status: open)

  ddx-95ec5ed5 is still open. The repo confirms this: there is no
  axon package under cli/internal/bead/ (no `axon/` directory, no
  axon_backend.go), no generated gRPC client (no `cli/internal/bead/
  axon/pb/`), no `axon.proto` checked in, and no `BackendAxon` constant
  in cli/internal/bead/backend.go (the file still lists only
  BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR and a 4-method Backend interface
  WriteAll/ReadAll/Init/WithLock — i.e. the pre-TD-030 surface).

  Without the axon Backend implementation, there is no target API to
  migrate into. Per TD-030 §"Migration plan" the migration command
  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`
  against the axon backend; none of those entry points exist in the
  Go tree yet. Building the migration tool now would require either:
    (a) inventing the axon backend in this same pass — out of scope,
        and ddx-95ec5ed5 is sized as its own bead, or
    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be
        churned once ddx-95ec5ed5 lands — wasted code that cannot be
        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  ddx-95ec5ed5 (and its upstreams ddx-bbdd7564 and ddx-6bb51a52) must
  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory
  (cli/internal/bead/registry.go) and exposes the per-entity surface
  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification
  pass (count parity + sampled hash comparison). Idempotency falls
  out of OCC presence-checks as TD-030 already specifies.

  The bead's dependency graph should be enforced in the queue: this
  bead should not become eligible until ddx-95ec5ed5 is closed. If
  the dependency was not declared via `ddx bead dep add` on this
  bead's id (only the textual description names it), that link
  should be added so `ddx bead ready` does not surface this work
  prematurely.

description:
LAYER 3 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. Completeness work — fixes incomplete Relay spec compliance.

PROBLEM
cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.

acceptance:
1. ExecuteBeadReport gains an explicit Disrupted bool (or Disposition variant) set when the loop detects: ctx.Err() != nil, executor exited via SIGKILL/SIGTERM, transport-class provider error, or worker process restart marker.
2. Detection at: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go where ExecutionFailed is set (current lines 513 routing-preflight, 629/637 executor-error, 1011 in execute_bead.go) — each set point evaluates whether the failure is Disrupted.
3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.
4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.
7. Tests:
   - TestLoop_DisruptedExecution_NoCooldown — context cancelled mid-execution; assert no execute-loop-retry-after set; bead returns to ready
   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set
   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)
   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events
8. Manual verification: kill an in-flight worker mid-execution; assert the bead is immediately re-claimable by the next worker (no 6h park).
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.
description:
The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.

acceptance: 1. All 15 children closed. 2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run replaced by work.Worker.Drain (~30 lines). 3. 18 store-error-as-loop-exit smells eliminated; transient store error during outcome-commit logs and continues. 4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result. 5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration. 6. ddx work default --poll-interval = 30s; --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 preserved for back-compat. 7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists. 8. File/identifier rename per §3 complete; ExecuteBead* shims deleted. 9. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically; tests green at every phase. 11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075 behaviors preserved per C5/C9 absorption ACs. 12. FEAT-006 + FEAT-010 cross-refs updated to reflect new package paths.
description:
Per Opus 4.7 review at /tmp/execute-bead-refactor-proposal.md.

ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate, 1 the poll-interval-zero exit, 1 the routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated 14 times. The user undercounted the smell by ~5×. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick → Gate → Claim → Execute → Classify → Reviewing → Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   — Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   — Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  — Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes ~30 lines: pick → guard → claim → attemptWithHeartbeat → applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming

Package = try (not try_bead — Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface (ddx run / try / work) already correct; C13 also ADDS ddx try as a new CLI command (FEAT-010 named it but it doesn't exist yet).

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e ladder + ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost + ddx-dc157075 tactical work-stay-alive). Includes downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes 30s default for ddx work --poll-interval.
  C10 ddx work stay-alive default + transient idle substate (subsumes ddx-dc157075 fully)
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per §3 of proposal + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)
title: REFACTOR: execute-bead surface → try / work packages aligned to FEAT-010

acceptance: 1. TD authored for the axon-backend integration (data model mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, multi-machine concurrency story). 2. Axon-backend implementation lands behind a feature flag or selectable backend in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. 3. Migration tool moves an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl into the axon backend without data loss. 4. Existing chaos_test.go suite passes against the axon backend. 5. bd fallback documented (single artifact under docs/) showing how to wire bd as the backend if axon adoption stalls. 6. Performance: queue ops (ready/blocked/show) at parity or better than JSONL on the current 1100-bead archive scale.
description:
The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).
notes: decomposed into ddx-6bb51a52 (TD), ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface), ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon impl), ddx-cb63cdfc (migration tool), ddx-743bc194 (chaos tests), ddx-8835a765 (bd fallback doc), ddx-6602559c (perf bench). Original epic was too big: 6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (design, refactor, new impl, migration, tests, docs, perf). Children form a DAG: TD → interface → {axon impl, bd doc}; axon impl → {migration, chaos, perf}.
title: Evaluate and adopt a real backend for the bead tracker (axon primary, bd fallback)

acceptance: 1. Server acquires flock on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock at startup. 2. Second 'ddx server' launch refuses with clear error message naming the existing pid + addr; exits non-zero. 3. Lock release on graceful shutdown (SIGTERM); stale-lock detection via dead-pid check on next startup. 4. writeAddrFile only runs after lock acquisition (file is consistent with lock holder). 5. ReadServerAddr (or its callers) validates recorded pid is alive before connecting. 6. If pid is dead, CLI emits a warning and falls back to default 127.0.0.1:7743. 7. New tests in cli/internal/server/: TestServer_SingletonGuardRefusesSecondLaunch + TestServer_StaleAddressFallbackWithWarning. 8. Manual verification: kill the live server; launch a new one; address-file matches the new server. Launch a second 'ddx server' while first is running; second refuses. 'ddx server workers list' succeeds against the live server after this fix.
description:
Two related bugs causing 'ddx server workers list' (and other server-talking CLI commands) to fail with 'connection refused dial tcp 127.0.0.1:<stale-port>' even when a live server is running.

## Diagnosis

**writeAddrFile already exists** at cli/internal/server/server.go:287-312 and is called from line 273 on every startup. So the file IS written. The real bugs are:

**Bug A: No singleton-server guard.** Multiple 'ddx server' instances can start on the same machine concurrently. Each one calls writeAddrFile and overwrites the previous record. When one dies (e.g., a manual launch test), it leaves the address-file pointing to itself even though it's no longer running. systemd unit (Type=simple) prevents systemd-spawned duplicates of the same unit name but does NOT stop a manual 'ddx server' from racing.

Observed today: live systemd server PID 3442304 listening on 7743 (started ~14:27), but address file shows PID 3482077 / port 4174 (started ~15:01, died). Some manual or test launch overwrote the file then died.

**Bug B: CLI doesn't validate pid-alive before trusting the address file.** ReadServerAddr at cli/internal/server/server.go:328+ returns whatever's in the file. CLI commands then dial the recorded URL. If the recorded pid is dead, dial fails. No fallback to the systemd default (127.0.0.1:7743), no warning.

## Fix

Two halves, same surface (server bootstrap + CLI client read path):

**Server-side (singleton guard):**
- On startup, server acquires flock on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock
- If lock is held by an alive process: refuse to start with clear error ('ddx server already running, pid=<N>, addr=<URL>; only one ddx server per machine'). Exit non-zero.
- If lock is acquired (or stale — held by dead pid): proceed; release-on-exit handler clears the lock.
- writeAddrFile happens AFTER the lock acquires — so the address file always corresponds to the lock-holder.

**Client-side (defense in depth):**
- ReadServerAddr (or its callers) validates the recorded pid is alive (kill -0 equivalent on Unix, OpenProcess on Windows)
- If pid is dead: emit warning ('server.addr points to a dead pid <N>; falling back to default 127.0.0.1:7743') and use the default
- Optionally clear the stale file (best-effort; don't fail if unwritable)

## Out of scope

- Multi-server-per-machine support (the singleton guard is intentional — one server per machine is the design per FEAT-020)
- Federation: hub/spoke topology (Story 14) is multi-node, NOT multi-server-per-node
notes:
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no code changes implementing the singleton flock guard, writeAddrFile ordering, pid-alive validation, or the required tests. None of the 8 acceptance criteria are satisfied by this diff.
title: server: singleton-per-machine guard + CLI validates pid-alive before trusting server.addr
