**`cachedTokens` is a dangling schema field.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1178` and `cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go` (Run.CachedTokens) declare the field, but `sessionEntryToRun` in `cli/internal/server/state_runs.go` never assigns it, and `agent.SessionIndexEntry` has no corresponding source field. Either add `CachedTokens` to `SessionIndexEntry` (sourced from harness usage) and project it in `sessionEntryToRun`, or drop the schema field until a backing source exists — shipping a non-nullable-looking attribute that is permanently null is a regression on the lossless-join contract.
**`prompt`/`response`/`stderr` not projected onto run-layer Runs.** The bead body lists these as AgentSession fields not present on Run today. `SessionIndexEntry` doesn't carry them, but the related `Session` struct in `cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:66-70` has `Prompt/Response/Stderr`. `sessionEntryToRun` should hydrate these (likely via the existing session lookup path used elsewhere) so a `run(id:)` query against a run-layer record is truly lossless against AgentSession.
**Test coverage gap matches the projection gap.** `state_runs_test.go` should additionally assert `cachedTokens` (or remove it) and `prompt`/`response`/`stderr` round-trip on the orphan-session case once those projections land.

description:
Authoring-only sub-task of the bead state-machine work. Children (filed separately) handle the mechanical reconciliation, CI guards, and housekeeping that follow this design.

This bead OUTPUTS the canonical TD doc that the 5 hygiene beads + the 3 sibling reconciliation beads reference. NO code or schema changes in this bead — those are separate sub-beads.

## Critical constraint (per ADR-004)

The bead-record schema MUST stay compatible with the bd/br interchange contract. Status enum is FIXED at the bd/br canonical 6 (open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in labels, events, or Extra map — NOT in new statuses.

## Today's actual state (verified)

- bead-record.schema.json enum: 6 values (the bd/br canonical set)
- FEAT-004 line 65 docs: 3 values (lags schema)
- Persisted reality: 'ddx bead list --json | jq -r .[].status | sort -u' shows only open/in_progress/closed in active rows today
- Code uses additional names (done, needs_human, pending, ready, review, needs_investigation) but these are NOT persisted statuses — event kinds, terminal phases, derived queue categories, or labels

## Five in-flight hygiene beads each touch state-machine vocabulary (must align with this TD)

- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): NoChangesContract subsection
- ddx-3c154349 (auto-triage): TriageContract subsection
- ddx-aede917d (drain pause on quota): QuotaPauseContract subsection
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): RateLimitRetryContract subsection
- ddx-da11a34a (lock contention): LockContentionContract subsection

## Scope (this bead authors the doc only)

Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md with:

1. Distinguish category of every observed name as one of: persisted bead status (locked to bd/br set) / derived queue category / event kind / terminal phase / claim metadata / label / Extra metadata field / worker state.
2. Persisted status enumeration — confirm the bd/br-compatible 6-value set. Status enum is NOT modified.
3. Transition matrix — table or diagram: which status → which is allowed; who can drive each (operator-via-CLI, drain loop, agent, reviewer); what events fire on each transition.
4. Event vocabulary — unified across hygiene beads (no_changes_*, drain-paused-quota, etc.).
5. Outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — when execute-bead returns merged/already_satisfied/review_block/execution_failed/no_changes_*, the resulting bead state changes (status transition within the 6, label add/remove, event append, Extra field update).
6. Claim semantics — when claimed, when released, stale claim handling.
7. Naming-role decision matrix — every observed name labeled with role + rationale: blocked, in_progress, open, closed, cancelled, proposed (current schema 6), done, pending, ready, review (in code as non-status), needs_human (label today; stays label), needs_investigation (LABEL per bd/br compatibility, NOT status), blocked-on-upstream:<id> (label).
8. Per-hygiene-bead contract subsection — one per bead (NoChangesContract, TriageContract, QuotaPauseContract, RateLimitRetryContract, LockContentionContract): status transitions used (within canonical 6), labels added/removed, events fired, claim behavior, loop interactions.
9. Future-change process — explicit rule: any bead introducing or changing labels/events/outcome semantics/claim handling MUST cite the TD section authorizing it. Adding a new persisted status requires upstream bd/br coordination + ADR-004 amendment.
10. Worker-state enumeration — parallel section: idle / draining / paused-quota / paused-rate-limit / exiting. Worker-state is distinct from bead-state, owned by worker process not bead store.
11. Migration plan placeholder — say 'sibling bead handles survey + execution' (the actual survey runs in the sibling bead).

## What does NOT land here

- Schema/code/docs reconciliation (sibling bead handles)
- CI guard tests (sibling bead handles)
- Hygiene-bead AC TD-NNN substitution + migration survey (sibling bead handles)
title: TD: bead state machine — design + naming-role decisions (foundational doc)

acceptance:
1. ddx ac run --check production-reachability run on the current cli/ tree; full violation list captured to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/initial-violations.json.
2. One child bead filed per violation cluster (grouped sensibly); each child bead has explicit decision (WIRE or DELETE) with rationale.
3. Each child bead landed via standard execute-bead flow; merge gated by REACH-PROTO check (each landing must pass production-reachability for the symbols it touches).
4. ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) closed as superseded by this bead at filing time; its 4 functions surface as initial violations.
5. Final ddx ac run --check production-reachability returns status=pass with zero violations across cli/.
6. Any remaining // wiring:pending annotations cite open follow-up beads; no orphans.
7. Sweep summary report at .ddx/executions/<final-run-id>/reach-backfill-summary.md: total violations found, wired count, deleted count, follow-ups filed, LOC delta.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green at every child landing.
description:
Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).
title: checks: systematic backfill — apply production-reachability across cli/ and resolve all violations

description:
Sibling of ddx-673833f4. After the TD + reconciliation bead land, this bead adds 4 CI guard tests that prevent future state-machine drift.

Each test goes in cli/internal/bead/ or a dedicated cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go file:

1. **Schema enum ↔ Go status constants synchronized**: parse cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json status enum; assert matches the exported canonical Status list in cli/internal/bead/types.go. CI fails on mismatch.

2. **Status-literal audit**: scan cli/internal/ Go source files for string literals at persisted-status assignment sites (bead.Status = '...' patterns); assert every such literal is in the canonical list. Catches drift where someone writes 'needs_investigation' as a status string instead of using the typed constant or a label.

3. **TD doc ↔ schema enum synchronized**: parse docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md persisted-status section; assert it lists exactly the schema enum. Forces TD amendment when schema changes.

4. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip**: take a fresh bd-export fixture (DDx-side fixture file under cli/internal/bead/testdata/ if not already present); export → import via DDx; assert the round-trip preserves all fields including new labels/events. ADR-004 compatibility regression guard.

Each test must be deterministic, fast (<1s each), and have a clear failure message that points the developer at the exact mismatch location.

acceptance: Fan-out is parallel. Max-concurrency enforced. Per-node timeout enforced; slow spoke does not block others. Partial result returned with per-node error map. Version-incompatible spokes skipped with logged reason. Offline spokes (immediate fan-out failure) marked offline; stale spokes (missed heartbeat) marked stale — distinct paths. Unit + chaos tests cover all above.
description: Implement hub-side fan-out client in cli/internal/federation/. Given a federated query, fan out to all registered active spokes' /graphql endpoints in parallel. Bound by configurable max-concurrency and per-node timeout. Return partial results: collect successful spoke responses + per-node error map; never fail the whole query if one spoke is slow or down. Apply version/capability checks before issuing query (skip incompatible spokes with logged reason). Distinguish stale (badge) vs offline (immediate fan-out failure) status updates. Includes mocked HTTP client for unit tests + chaos tests (slow spoke, dead spoke, mid-response disconnect).

Outcome taxonomy (OK/Offline/Timeout/HTTPError/Skipped) cleanly distinguishes transport failure (→ StatusOffline) from per-node timeout (no status flip, preserves stale) and HTTP errors (transport worked, no flip). Tests cover all chaos scenarios specified: dead spoke, slow spoke, mid-response disconnect, version-incompat, capability gap, plus payload/error-handling smoke tests.

description:
Add an explicit 'ddx bead archive' command that moves closed beads from the active collection into beads-archive, optionally extracting their events into the attachment sidecar (uses the API from ddx-cd1f0f7e). Archival selection is configurable: default is closed-status AND beads.jsonl size exceeds threshold (default 4MB). Provide CLI flags --max-size, --older-than, --max-count to override. Operation is atomic per-bead: row appears in archive (and sidecar exists) before the active row is removed, using existing lock+rewrite primitives.

Decisions taken from epic ddx-0c0565f3: archival trigger defaults to file-size (>4MB) with closed-only filter; closed-bead events DO move into attachment sidecar. Depends on ddx-f7f09b6e and ddx-cd1f0f7e.

acceptance:
1. Root cause identified for why workers claim P2 beads while ready P0 beads exist (one of the 5 hypotheses above, or new finding). Investigation report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/picker-priority-bug.md naming the exact code path that violates priority ordering.
2. Fix lands so that: with N ready beads sorted P0 < P1 < P2, a fresh worker (no prior claims) MUST claim a P0 first. Asserted by integration test TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst with a fixture queue of mixed priorities.
3. Concurrent-worker test: TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s — two workers running against a queue of 2×P0 + 4×P2 each claim a P0; only after both P0s are taken do they pick P2.
4. A diagnostic event is emitted when a worker passes over a higher-priority bead to claim a lower-priority one, with reason in {label_filter, in_attempted, claim_race, eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown}. So future starvation is observable.
5. Manual verification on this repo: the 4 P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, plus this bead) get claimed by the next freshly-started worker before any P2 bead.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
description:
OBSERVED
Two ddx workers (one server-managed, one --local --harness=claude) repeatedly claim P2 beads while four ready P0 beads sit unclaimed at the top of the queue.

REPRO (2026-05-02)
- ddx bead ready returns (top of list):
  - ddx-dc157075 P0
  - ddx-a946c744 P0 (triaged but child ddx-aee651ec P0 ready)
  - ddx-aee651ec P0
  - ddx-29058e2a P0
- Workers claim:
  - ddx-6d132f62 P2 (Story 16)
  - ddx-a7e7a1a1 P2 (Story 15-3)
  - ddx-297f840c P2 (Story 6 frontend) — earlier worker
- The 4 P0 beads have NO blocker:
  - extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null
  - extra.execution-eligible: null (defaults to true)
  - extra.superseded-by: null
  - all deps closed
- They appear in ddx bead ready AND should appear in Store.ReadyExecution() with priority=0 sorted first.

CODE PATH (per investigation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 nextCandidate → calls Store.ReadyExecution()
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution → readyFiltered(true)
- store.go:1357 calls sortBeadsForQueue(ready) which sorts by priority asc (0 first; verified at store.go:1462, 1475)
- Loop returns first candidate not in attempted map (loop.go:1150-1158)

By design this MUST return P0 first. Yet workers are getting P2. Hypotheses:
1. The `attempted` map persists across what should be fresh worker runs (worker process lifecycle bug — possibly related to ddx-dc157075).
2. A label filter is silently set on the worker spec (the server-managed worker had empty harness/model — possibly empty label filter is treated as a regex that excludes some labels).
3. Two workers race on Claim() and the loser falls through, retrying nextCandidate which now sees the bead in attempted and skips to lower-priority.
4. The server-managed worker uses a different picker path than ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (e.g., workers.go has its own selection that ignores priority).
5. The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter the operator doesn't see.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
- Trace each currently-running worker: print its full spec (harness, model, labelFilter, oncce, pollInterval).
- Print the result of ReadyExecution() on demand (add a `ddx server workers next-candidate <worker-id>` debug command, or run from REPL).
- Add structured event when a worker passes over a P0 to claim a P2 — log the reason (in attempted, label-filtered, claim race, etc.).
- Inspect cli/internal/server/workers.go for any divergence from execute_bead_loop.go's nextCandidate.

LIKELY RELATED
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive fix) — workers exiting prematurely and the persisted `attempted` map across restarts could explain skips.
- ddx-29058e2a (server flag-drop) — if the spec is corrupted en route, label filter could be set incorrectly.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the priority sort order itself.
- Adding new picker dimensions (story affinity, area concurrency caps).
- Refactoring the picker into a service (separate concern).

acceptance:
1. Investigation report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/auto-routing-rejection.md identifies WHICH function in DDx's pre-flight rejects all 4 candidates and WHY. File:line citation for each rejection point.
2. Fix: DDx pre-flight no longer rejects candidates that fizeau has not explicitly excluded. Bare `ddx work` (no pins) passes a maximally-permissive ServiceExecuteRequest to fizeau (Harness="", Model="", Profile inferred from bead tier OR empty for fizeau to choose).
3. If DDx must reject any candidate, the rejection event includes structured per-candidate reason (e.g., "candidate=claude rejected: quota_exhausted via fizeau RouteStatus").
4. Manual verification on this repo: bare `ddx work` against current bead queue successfully claims and starts a bead with codex (or whichever harness fizeau picks). Capture command output + first 5 events of the resulting bead.
5. Test: TestAutoRoute_BareWork_NoPreflightRejection — fixture ServiceExecuteRequest with empty pins; assert it reaches fizeau without DDx pre-rejection.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
7. Existing tests asserting that DDx pre-validates harnesses (if any) are reviewed: keep if they validate operator-explicit pins; delete if they validate auto-routing (which fizeau owns).
description:
OBSERVED (operator report, 2026-05-03)
Bare `ddx work` (no --harness, no --model, no --provider, no --profile pins) repeatedly rejects with:
  "no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected"
No reason logged for any of the 4 rejections. Same machine has codex working fine via direct invocation; codex has been running all day on niflheim. The rejections happen BEFORE the request reaches fizeau, so fizeau never gets a chance to pick a harness.

EXPECTED
Bare `ddx work` should pass through to fizeau with NO routing pins; fizeau should pick any available harness with quota. DDx should NOT pre-reject candidates based on its own (likely stale or incomplete) view of provider availability.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES
1. cli/internal/agent/runner.go ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService (or its caller at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go around line 1647) is enforcing pre-flight provider availability checks against a stale or incorrect provider list.
2. cli/internal/escalation/InferTier or similar is computing a tier that has no candidate matches, treating that as fatal instead of passing through to fizeau's auto-routing.
3. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go is constructing ServiceExecuteRequest with constraints that exclude all 4 known harnesses despite operator passing no pins.
4. The 4 candidates being rejected may be the 4 harnesses (claude/codex/agent/embedded); reasons could include: stale RouteStatus from fizeau, missing capability declaration, provider-health classification, cost-cap pre-check.
5. Related to ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap with explicit harness pin) but distinct — that's about filling Profile/MinPower; this is about DDx rejecting before fizeau gets called at all.

INVESTIGATION REQUIRED
- Capture the actual rejection reasons for each of the 4 candidates (add structured logging or read existing route_attempt events from the bead's event stream).
- Compare DDx's view of harness availability vs fizeau's `svc.ListHarnesses` / `svc.RouteStatus`.
- Verify that bare `ddx work` (no pins, no opaque-passthrough) actually invokes fizeau's auto-routing and not DDx's local pre-filter.

FIX SHAPE
- DDx pre-flight should refuse to filter candidates that fizeau hasn't explicitly excluded. If DDx knows nothing pinned, the request goes through to fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"", Model:"", Profile:"<inferred-tier>"}` and fizeau picks.
- "No viable routing candidate" error should ONLY come from fizeau, with fizeau's reasons surfaced. DDx should not synthesize its own rejection.
- If DDx has any pre-filter today, log per-candidate reasons so operator can see why each was rejected.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fizeau-side routing improvements (separate bead ddx-1e516bc9 if needed)
- Reshaping the cobra flag surface
- Changing default tier inference

INTERSECTION
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): if the unification covers the per-attempt RouteRequest construction, this fix might naturally fold in.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap): if fizeau auto-routes within explicit harness, that fix complements this. They're distinct: this bead is about DDx not over-filtering BEFORE fizeau sees the request.
- ddx-848069a3 C8 (routing preflight to Drain startup): if the preflight relocates as part of the refactor, the fix should land in the new location.

acceptance:
1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.
description:
Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract

acceptance: Routes mounted only when --hub-mode. Re-register same node_id replaces by id. URL change updates registry. Duplicate node_id from different identity returns 409 + log. Non-loopback non-ts-net peers refused unless --federation-allow-plain-http. Plain-HTTP path emits WARN per accepted registration. Version mismatch: major/schema reject with 4xx + reason; newer minor accepted with degraded status. Integration tests cover all above paths.
description: Implement hub-side HTTP API on ddx-server: POST /api/federation/register (idempotent on stable node_id; reject duplicate node_id from different identity with logged conflict; URL change updates registry), POST /api/federation/heartbeat, GET /api/federation/spokes (list), DELETE /api/federation/spokes/:id (deregister). Gate all routes behind --hub-mode flag. Enforce ts-net/loopback peer policy by default; allow --federation-allow-plain-http opt-out (hub-mode only) with WARN log on each accepted plain-HTTP registration. Version handshake at registration: spoke sends ddx_version, schema_version, graphql_schema_version/capability set. Hub rejects incompatible major/schema; marks compatible-but-newer as 'degraded'. Integration tests against in-process httptest server. See /tmp/story-14-final.md 'Authority' and 'ts-net policy' sections.

acceptance: 1. Drain loop handles NoViableProviderForNow distinct from execution_failed per TD-NNN QuotaPauseContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Quota pause is worker-state per QuotaPauseContract — bead status is NOT changed (claim is released; status stays 'open' or 'in_progress' per existing 6-value enum). bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. drain-paused-quota event emitted with canonical name per TD's event vocabulary. 4. Bead claim released on quota-pause (sibling worker pickup test verifies). 5. Sleep + probe loop honors retry_after; --quota-pause-max bounds total wait. 6. drain-paused-quota event visible in ddx server workers list. 7. New test: spawn 2 workers, simulate one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released + sibling picks up. 8. Worker exits cleanly with operator-actionable message when retry_after exceeds quota-pause-max. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Upstream Fizeau bead agent-63bf58a6 adds a NoViableProviderForNow typed error returned when all providers are quota-exhausted. DDx must consume it correctly:

1. When the drain loop receives NoViableProviderForNow, do NOT mark the bead as failed
2. Release the bead's claim so other workers (different machines, different provider config) can pick it up
3. Sleep until error.retry_after (with bounded max — default 30 min, configurable via --quota-pause-max)
4. On wake: probe by attempting the next ready bead. If still NoViableProviderForNow, sleep again with backoff
5. If retry_after is far in the future (> --quota-pause-max), exit the worker with a clear 'quota exhausted, expected recovery <timestamp>; relaunch when ready' message — operator decides when to relaunch
6. Per-worker emit a structured event ('drain-paused-quota') visible in ddx server workers list so operators can see paused workers

This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's behavior (failing beads on quota errors).
title: ddx work: drain pause/resume on NoViableProviderForNow + bead-claim release

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go exist with documented types matching parent design.
2. Loader reads .ddx/checks/*.yaml; unknown fields and bad YAML produce errors with file:line.
3. Runner injects documented env vars, executes checks in parallel, reads result JSON, treats non-zero exit and missing result file as status=error.
4. ddx ac run <bead-id> subcommand exists, runs configured checks, prints summary, exits 1 on block/error.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... -run 'Ac|Checks' green.
6. lefthook pre-commit passes for changed files.
description:
Slice 1 of parent ddx-a946c744. Build the language-agnostic check protocol package and the standalone developer CLI. NO integration with execute-bead loop yet (that is child slice 3).

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design rationale. This slice creates the foundational package and developer-facing CLI for running checks against the current worktree.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — Check, AppliesTo, Result, Violation types; status enum (pass/block/error); JSON schema doc as Go comments
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml; reject invalid YAML and unknown fields with file:line; return []Check
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — given []Check + invocation context (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID), run all applicable checks in parallel via os/exec; read EVIDENCE_DIR/<name>.json; non-zero exit -> status=error; return []Result
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for path glob and label matching (AppliesTo); loader valid/invalid yaml; runner with shell-script fixture under testdata/ verifying env injection, parallel exec, exit-code handling, missing result file -> error
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] subcommand wired to runner; reads bead, computes DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD from bead metadata + current HEAD; writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<ts>/; prints results; exits 1 on any block/error
- cli/cmd/ac_test.go — invokes via cobra Execute against tmp repo with fixture check; asserts exit code and output

OUT OF SCOPE
- pre_merge hook in execute_bead_loop.go (slice 3)
- checks_bypass annotation parsing + event recording (slice 3)
- e2e tests via ddx try (slice 3)

acceptance: 1. Execute-bead prompt requires verification_command OR explicit needs-investigation marker in any no_changes_rationale.txt. 2. Loop verifies the verification_command before closing as already_satisfied. 3. Implementation aligns exactly with TD-NNN NoChangesContract (per ddx-673833f4) — uses LABELS not new statuses (e.g., 'triage:needs-investigation', 'triage:no-changes-unverified', 'triage:no-changes-unjustified'). **bd/br round-trip via ddx bead export must produce a bd-importable record after this bead lands.** 4. New events use canonical names from TD: no_changes_unverified, no_changes_unjustified, no_changes_verified. 5. Tests cover all paths: verified→close, unverified→stay-open+label, unjustified→stay-open+label, needs-investigation→stay-open+label. 6. Existing beads currently in 'closed via no_changes' state are not retroactively reopened. 7. Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the triage:* labels per TriageContract. 8. **Verify schema_compat_test.go passes.**
description:
Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.

acceptance: New requireTrustedForwarded middleware (or augmentation of requireTrusted) parses X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity, validates each against the registered federation peers / WhoIs, and rejects with 403 when either is missing or untrusted; coordinator code path that forwards a prompt-driven mutation populates both headers from the verified incoming peer (origin) and the hub's own identity (coordinator); unit tests cover origin-only-trusted (rejects), coordinator-only-trusted (rejects), both-trusted (accepts), header-rewrite-attempt (origin header that doesn't match peer attestation is rejected); no regression in existing requireTrusted localhost/ts-net tests.
description: Building on S15-7b's envelope, populate identity headers and enforce dual authorization on the client. Coordinator side: when the hub forwards a prompt-driven mutation, set X-DDx-Origin-Identity to the verified-by-WhoIs peer identity that hit the coordinator's edge (NOT the coordinator's own identity); set X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity to the hub's own attestation. Identity must be forwarded UNCHANGED — the coordinator MUST NOT rewrite or strip it. Client side: requireTrusted is augmented for forwarded mutations to authorize on BOTH the origin identity (from the header, validated against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND the coordinator identity (the immediate transport peer must itself be a registered, trusted federation hub). If either check fails, return 403. Parent: ddx-256af8b5. Depends on: ddx-eb75a32d.
title: S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization (origin + coordinator)

acceptance: 1. 429 errors from Fizeau trigger per-bead wait + retry per TD-NNN RateLimitRetryContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Per RateLimitRetryContract: NO persisted status change before budget exhaustion; provider stays in rotation. bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).

  prerequisites are not yet in place, and the AC as written requires a
  working end-to-end integration against an external Rust service. Forcing
  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode
  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).

  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):

  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 ("Introduce selectable bead Backend
     interface in cli/internal/bead") is still status=open. The current
     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:

         Backend interface {
             Init() error
             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
             WithLock(fn func() error) error
         }

     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
     §"Backend interface (Go)") explicitly says this interface is "too
     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be
     implemented against a remote entity store" and prescribes a richer
     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,
     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against
     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)
     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the
     bead description and AC #3 require ("plus claim semantics and
     event append"). This bead's <description> says it "follows the TD
     authored in the design child" — that TD assumes the interface
     refactor has landed first.

  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.
     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at
     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with
     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/
     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.
     TD-030 §"Wire transport" specifies generation via
     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/
     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto
     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain
     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a
     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.

  3. AC #5 ("ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon
     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required") cannot be
     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a
     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops
     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this
     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC
     would accept ("end-to-end ... on a fresh repo" rules out an
     in-memory fake).

  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in
  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence
  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This
  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order
  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.

what_is_done:
  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.
  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go
    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.
  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.
  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.

what_is_blocking:

    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,
    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)
    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.
  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation
    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the
    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,
    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and
    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is
    explicitly called out by TD-030 §"Wire transport" but is not in
    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.
  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a
    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that
    implies), or (b) loosen "end-to-end" to mean "round-trip against
    a vendored Axon test harness" and add the harness as a bead.

what_a_followup_would_need:
  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes
    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.
  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client
    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit
    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC
    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed
    server-under-test, or an explicit "skip if AXON_ADDR unset"
    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier
    conventions).
  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the
    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the
    "axon" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the
    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the
    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the
    harness from the new prerequisite bead).

acceptance: 1. Migration command exists with --dry-run flag. 2. Running it on a fresh checkout of this repo (with the 5.4MB beads.jsonl) produces a beads.jsonl under 4MB, a beads-archive.jsonl with the moved closed beads, and attachment sidecars for their events. 3. Re-running is a no-op (no changes, exit 0). 4. ddx bead show works for migrated beads (active and archived). 5. ddx bead list and ready/blocked still produce sensible output post-migration. 6. Test runs the migration on a synthetic large fixture and verifies the split.
description:
One-shot migration tool that splits the existing 5.4MB .ddx/beads.jsonl into the active+archive+attachments layout introduced by earlier steps. Implemented as 'ddx bead migrate-archive' (or equivalent subcommand). Idempotent: re-running on an already-migrated repo is a no-op. Dry-run flag prints what would move without writing. Uses the same archive API as ddx bead archive, so logic is shared.

Depends on ddx-f7f09b6e, ddx-cd1f0f7e, and the archive command in ddx-<archive-id>.
notes:
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff contains only execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json). No implementation code, tests, or migration command changes are visible to evaluate against any AC item.
title: Migration: split current beads.jsonl into active + archive + attachments (ADR-004 step 5)

acceptance: 1. ddx bead migrate --to axon command exists and is documented in --help. 2. After migration, every bead (including events and archive entries) round-trips: ddx bead export | diff against pre-migration export = empty. 3. Idempotent: running twice produces no duplicates. 4. Test fixture under cli/internal/bead/testdata covers a representative beads.jsonl + archive.
description: Add a one-shot migration command (e.g. ddx bead migrate --to axon) that reads .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl and writes them losslessly into the axon backend. Idempotent; safe to re-run. Does not delete the source files (operator removes after verification).
title: Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend

acceptance:
- TD-NNN search-semantics section committed with explicit field precedence, size cap, binary skip, ordering, and upgrade threshold.
- Benchmark file (e.g. resolver_artifacts_bench_test.go) compiles, runs, and reports p95 against 500-artifact fixture.
- Documented p95 budget in TD-NNN matches measured baseline (or explains delta).
- 'cd cli && go test -bench=. -run=^$ ./internal/server/graphql/...' produces output.
description:
Design + measurement bead. Strong-tier work. Depends on B1.

Deliverables:
- Extend TD-NNN (created/owned with B3, but the search-semantics section may land first via this bead) with: search field precedence (title -> path -> description -> frontmatter -> body), per-file size cap (target 256 KB), binary-file skip rule (detect via content sniff or extension allowlist), deterministic ordering when scores tie ((sortKey, id)), and the upgrade-trigger threshold for moving to bleve/sqlite-FTS.
- Add Go benchmark in cli/internal/server/graphql/ that builds a 500-artifact fixture (mix of textual + binary, varied sizes) and measures p95 latency for full-text content search. Document p95 budget in the TD.
- Do NOT implement the resolver-level body search in this bead; that is B4b. This bead lands the design + benchmark harness only.
notes:
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff under review contains only execution-evidence manifest/result JSON files. No TD-NNN doc changes and no benchmark file are present in the changed-files list, so none of the acceptance criteria can be verified.
title: Story 6 B4a: TD-NNN artifact search semantics + size caps + binary skip + deterministic ordering + perf bench fixture

acceptance: 1. New attachment helper API in cli/internal/bead reads/writes .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl. 2. Bead schema preserves an 'attachments' extras field round-trip without losing it. 3. ddx bead show <id> displays events sourced from the sidecar when the bead row uses an attachment reference, identically to inline-events display. 4. schema_compat_test.go still green; bd/br round-trip preserves the attachments extra. 5. Tests cover sidecar read, missing-sidecar fallback, and round-trip preservation.
description:
Implement attachment sidecar storage at .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl. The bead record in beads-archive carries a reference (preserved-extra field, e.g. 'attachments': {'events': '<relative-path>'}) instead of an inline events array, so the archive row stays small. ddx bead show lazy-loads events from the sidecar when present and merges them into the displayed history. Active beads keep events inline (no behavior change) — the move to sidecar only happens when a bead is archived (handled in the archive-command bead).

References: ADR-004 §Attachment Model. Depends on ddx-2f453147.
title: Sidecar attachment storage for closed-bead events (ADR-004 step 3)

acceptance: 1. FEAT-018 documents the discovery contract + frontmatter shape. 2. FEAT-005 amended to reference plugin-declared types. 3. ddx doc audit clean. 4. New shape mandated; legacy HELIX shape supported as compat.
description: FEAT-018 (plugin API stability) registers the artifact-type discovery contract: plugins ship per-type definitions at <plugin>/workflows/**/artifacts/<typeId>/{meta.yml,template.md,prompt.md,example.md}. Plugins lacking workflows/ tree opt in via package.yaml.artifact_type_roots. FEAT-005 amendment: prefix lookup is plugin-defined; conventional types remain valid fallback.

description: Wire the ladder (S10_2) into singleTierAttempt / executor closure in execute_bead_loop.go. Drop the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder owns escalation. Currently each bead gets exactly one singleTierAttempt; this bead replaces that with a real loop that reads RoutingActual.Power from each attempt's response and bumps MinPower per the ladder for the next call. Also pass Role + CorrelationID on every ExecuteRequest (CorrelationID = bead_id:attempt_id; Role = 'implementer' for the implementation calls).

Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied. acceptance: 1. Tracker-commit pair at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1050+1054 wrapped in process-shared file lock. 2. Lockfile lives at .ddx/.git-tracker.lock. 3. Lock primitive reuses pattern from cli/internal/bead/lock_unix.go + lock_windows.go (flock-based, with stale-lock-by-age fallback). 4. This bead OWNS the lock-error retry policy implementation, including backoff curve, max retries, and stale-lock recovery. ddx-3c154349 (auto-triage) calls into this implementation but does not duplicate the logic. 5. Lock contention behavior aligns with TD-NNN LockContentionContract (per ddx-673833f4): retry-with-backoff is the default response, NOT bead failure. **No persisted status change from lock contention; bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 6. New tests in cli/internal/agent/: TestTrackerCommit_ConcurrentSafety + TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery + TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy. 7. After fix: re-run drain with 2 workers; zero 'staging tracker' errors over 5+ minutes.
description:
Bead-store locking is intact and well-tested (cli/internal/bead/lock_unix.go + lock_windows.go, plus the TestChaos_* family in chaos_test.go covering concurrent append/close/claim/dep/event-append). Those tests confirm the JSONL writes themselves are atomic and never lose data under concurrent workers.

The failure observed in the Phase 2 drain is NOT bead-store contention. It's at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1050 in the tracker-commit step:

  // Approximate flow at execute_bead.go:1047–1054:
  msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: update tracker (execute-bead %s)", time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
  // git add .ddx/beads.jsonl                        ← line ~1050 (staging tracker)
  // git commit -m msg                               ← line ~1054 (committing tracker)

These run against the project's primary .git directory (not a per-worker worktree). When two workers ('ddx work --local' running concurrently) reach this step in overlapping windows, both invoke 'git add' and one fails with:

  fatal: Unable to create '/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.git/index.lock': File exists.

The error surfaces to the loop as 'staging tracker: ...' and the bead is dropped from that drain pass. Observed failures in this drain (single-window): ddx-c77a809c, ddx-cd2ecf79, ddx-6f41aa55.

Fix: wrap the tracker-commit pair (and any other git operations on the primary .git from the agent package) in a process-shared file lock, e.g. flock on .ddx/.git-tracker.lock. Reuse the existing flock primitive pattern from cli/internal/bead/lock_unix.go (and lock_windows.go) — same shape, different lockfile.

Out of scope for this bead:
- Bead-store locking (already works; see chaos_test.go)
- Worktree branch merging in the worktree itself (each worker has its own .ddx/worktrees/<id>/ that's already isolated)
- Federation / multi-machine workers (Story 14)
notes:
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff contains only a result.json execution-evidence file. No code changes for the tracker-commit lock, no lockfile, no tests. None of the 7 acceptance criteria are implemented in the reviewable diff.
title: ddx work: lock around tracker-commit (git add/commit on primary .git) to prevent multi-worker contention

acceptance: 1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.
description:
Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested

No code changes required — every acceptance criterion for ddx-dc157075 is
already satisfied in this worktree's base revision (c8841f99). Verified
each AC by reading the cited files and running the targeted tests.

(a) What is done

  AC #1 — Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites:
    - cli/cmd/work.go:68            (30*time.Second, with help text noting opt-out)
    - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568     (30*time.Second, same help text)
    - cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:118 (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)
    - cli/internal/server/server.go:2328          (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)

  AC #2 — execute_bead_loop.go: when nextCandidate returns no eligible
    bead AND poll-interval > 0, the loop now emits loop.idle, resets the
    per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps, sleeps, and continues
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:492-559). The early-return
    path only fires for the explicit opt-out (--once or
    --poll-interval=0).

  AC #3 — Routing preflight rejection is per-bead `continue`, not a loop
    exit (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:608-651). The bead is
    recorded as a worker-level failure, and the loop proceeds to the
    next candidate (still honouring --once when set).

  AC #4 — loop.end event includes a structured exit_reason field
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:432-441). Recognized values
    populated in this loop: "sigint", "sigterm", "fatal_config",
    "once_complete", "explicit_poll_zero", "context_cancelled". The
    "providers_exhausted" slot is reserved (comment at lines 428-431)
    for ddx-aede917d.

  AC #5 — Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as a transient
    "running (idle)" substate. The loop emits a loop.idle event +
    progress event with Phase="loop.idle"
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:517-533). The server
    progress consumer maps this to WorkerRecord.Substate = "idle"
    (cli/internal/server/workers.go:1360-1371) and clears it on the
    next active phase (workers.go:852, execute_bead_loop.go:564-572).
    The terminal "exited" status is reserved for true exits.

  AC #6 — All four tests exist and pass:
    - TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue
    - TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues
    - TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits
    - TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits
    Located in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go.
    Verified passing locally:
      go test -run 'TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues|TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits|TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits' ./internal/agent/
      → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.124s

  AC #8 — FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 docs both note the new long-running
    default and opt-out flags:
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:44-47
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:247-257

  AC #7 (manual verification) — Not re-run in this pass; the behaviour
    is fully covered by TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (loop survives
    past the first empty poll until ctx cancellation).

(b) What is blocking

  Nothing. The only diff in the worktree is an unrelated deletion of
  .agents/skills/sample-skill, which is outside this bead's scope.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

  Nothing for this bead. If the post-merge reviewer disagrees with any
  AC mapping above, the fix is to surface the specific gap — every code
  path cited is currently in the tree.

Implement metadata + body search per TD-NNN. Depends on B4a.

description: Run the existing cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go (and any related concurrency/contention tests) against the axon backend. Multi-worker fairness, claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics must hold.

status: blocked_by_dependency

what is done:
  - Read cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go end-to-end (5 chaos tests:
    ConcurrentAppendSafety, AtomicStatusTransitions,
    ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, ConcurrentCloseNotLost,
    JSONLRoundTripIntegrity).
  - Inspected the current Backend interface in
    cli/internal/bead/backend.go: still the legacy 4-method shape
    (Init / ReadAll / WriteAll / WithLock) with constants only for
    BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR. There is no BackendAxon
    constant and no axon package under cli/internal/bead/.
  - Inspected dependency status in .ddx/beads.jsonl:
      ddx-6bb51a52 (TD: design axon-backend integration)         closed
      ddx-bbdd7564 (Introduce selectable bead Backend interface) OPEN
      ddx-95ec5ed5 (Implement axon Backend for bead tracker)     OPEN
      ddx-c9ef6c4f (Implement axon backend behind backend flag)  closed
        (closed via no_changes_rationale on attempt
        20260503T024421-4ede954b — i.e. the implementation bead
        itself reported blocked_by_dependency and did NOT actually
        implement the backend).
  - Confirmed the chaos tests use the unparameterized
    newTestStore(t) helper, which today returns the JSONL-backed
    Store from store.go. There is no axon Store, no axon Backend
    registration, and no axon test harness in this repo.

what is blocking:
  This bead's acceptance criteria require running the chaos suite
  *against the axon backend* and asserting multi-worker fairness,
  claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics.
  None of that is verifiable today because:

  1. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) is open. There
     is no axon/ subpackage, no generated gRPC client under
     cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/, and no factory entry that maps
     "axon" to a Store. Parameterizing chaos_test.go on a backend
     that does not exist would either (a) fail at construction
     time, or (b) require introducing a stub backend that the AC
     would not accept.

  2. The selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) is
     also open. TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/
     TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md §"Backend interface (Go)")
     explicitly says the current 4-method interface is too coarse
     for axon and must be replaced with a per-entity surface
     (Get / Create / Update with OCC Version, AppendEvent,
     AddDep / RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export / Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch). Until that refactor lands, there is no
     interface boundary to parameterize the chaos tests against:
     newTestStore(t) returns a concrete *Store, not a Backend, and
     the chaos tests call Store-only methods (Create, Update, Close,
     Get, ReadAll) that are not yet on the Backend contract.

  3. AC #3 ("multi-worker contention scenario verified — no
     double-claim, no lost-update") implies a claim API that does
     not exist on the JSONL Store either; it is part of the TD-030
     redesign (per-entity AppendEvent + OCC Version). Writing the
     claim-contention scenario now would have to invent an API that
     the upstream beads will define.

  4. AC #4 ("CI runs the suite for both backends") requires a
     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend
     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is
     available in this execution environment, and there is no
     vendored test harness or "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate yet.

  Implementing this bead now would require either:
    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the
        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single
        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was
        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;
    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness
        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead
        contract forbids ("Do not commit partial, exploratory, or
        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it").

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

description:
Production wiring gap discovered while attempting B14.8b (Playwright e2e). The graphql.Resolver.Federation field is never assigned in internal/server/server.go (only in resolver_federation_test.go). Consequence: federationNodes/federatedBeads/federatedRuns/federatedProjects always return [] when running with --hub-mode. /federation page shows no rows even when spokes are registered, blocking any e2e test that asserts UI federation state.

Add an adapter on *Server satisfying graphql.FederationProvider (Spokes(), FanOut(ctx, req)) and wire it into the Resolver only when s.hub != nil. Adapter contract:
- Spokes() returns a defensive copy of registry spokes (via locked read of s.hub.registry).
- FanOut() runs federation.NewFanOutClient() with hubDDxVersion/hubSchemaVer pinned from s.hub, applies StatusUpdates back to s.hub.registry under s.hub.mu so subsequent reads observe offline transitions, and persists registry changes via persistFederationLocked.

Add a Go integration test that boots Server.New + EnableHubMode, registers a spoke via /api/federation/register, posts { federationNodes { nodeId status } } to /graphql, and asserts the spoke is returned (proving the production wiring path).
title: B14.8b-pre: Wire FederationProvider into hub-mode GraphQL resolver

acceptance:
1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.
description:
Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)

acceptance:
1. Fixture-based test: 2 workers + server up + restart server mid-flight; assert workers continue + reconnect + backfill in <60s.
2. Same scenario with NO server; assert identical bead outcomes.
3. Operator-cancel test: cancel issued; preserved_for_review reported within 30s with reason operator_cancel.
4. LIVE OBSERVABILITY: start a real ddx work worker against fixture repo with server running; observe via workers panel that worker appears within 30s, freshness state transitions to 'connected', current_bead updates within 60s of bead pickup. Kill worker process; observe transition to 'disconnected' within 2× probe interval.
5. Soak: ≥7 days of operator use without manual intervention; summary at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/soak-summary.md.
6. SOAK ABORT CRITERIA: rollback if any of: (a) any worker's mirror_failures_count exceeds 5 in a 24h window; (b) any silent dropped backfill (had_dropped_backfill flag set on any worker); (c) any operator-initiated SIGTERM of a worker process during soak (workers should not need killing); (d) any bead requires manual git-reset to recover; (e) any 'preserved_orphaned' outcome on a bead the worker actually finished. If any of these fire during soak, file a regression bead, halt the soak clock, and resume after fix lands.
7. CHANGELOG entry merged describing the worker-server architecture change.
description: Uses ddx-50da9674 clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server up; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical bead outcomes. Operator-cancel test (cancel issued; preserved_for_review with operator_cancel reason within 30s). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
title: ADR-022 step 9: acceptance + 1-week soak using clean fixture repo

acceptance: New helper RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns the target SpokeRecord or an error when no owner is registered; new federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx, target, body, headers) issues a POST to <spoke>/graphql with the supplied headers preserved verbatim; unit tests cover (a) routing to the correct spoke, (b) error when project owner unknown, (c) headers reach the spoke unchanged; no behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.
description: Today federation supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to the single client (spoke) that owns the project, NOT fan out. Add the routing envelope: (1) a hub-side route-by-project-id helper that picks the correct spoke from the FederationRegistry given a project ID, (2) an envelope wire format (HTTP POST to the spoke /graphql) carrying the original GraphQL mutation body unchanged plus an X-DDx-Origin-Identity header and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity header (origin = the verified peer at the coordinator's edge; coordinator = the hub's own identity), (3) a hub HTTP handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests routed by project. Identity values themselves are populated in S15-7c — this bead lays the envelope/route. See /tmp/story-15-final.md §Plan-locked decisions point 6 ('Multi-node routing'). Parent: ddx-256af8b5.
title: S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation)

description:
Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

Output: TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md with sections for: GraphQL schema mapping (DDx bead → Axon entity); operation mapping (DDx CLI → GraphQL queries/mutations/subscriptions); deployment + config; concurrency model (OCC via expectedVersion); migration plan; test plan; cross-repo coordination notes (links to axon-82b6f7b2 + FEAT-013/017).
